title: Energy for America’s Economic Future Act
short_title: Energy for America’s Economic Future Act
plain_summary: This bill creates a special fund to help pay down the national debt. Every three months, the government must put 25% of the money it makes from selling oil and gas leases into this fund. The government must also put 25% of money it makes from artificial intelligence projects into the same fund. All this money must be used to buy back government bonds, which helps reduce how much the country owes.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Energy for America's Economic Future Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Debt Reduction Fund
sections.text: This section creates a special account to help pay down the national debt using money from energy leases. The Fund means the Debt Reduction Fund that this section creates. The Secretary means the Secretary of the Treasury (the person who manages the country's money). Total revenue means all the money collected when companies buy or rent federal land to drill for oil and gas, including the upfront payments, ongoing rental fees, and royalties paid over time. The government will create a new account in the Treasury called the Debt Reduction Fund. Starting one hundred days after this law passes, the government will put money into this fund every three months. Twenty-five percent of all money collected from federal oil and gas lease sales on land and offshore will go into the fund. This includes money from leases under several existing laws that govern mineral rights on federal lands. Twenty-five percent of all money collected from activities related to Executive Order fourteen thousand one hundred forty-one about artificial intelligence infrastructure will also go into the fund. The money in this fund can only be used to pay down the federal debt. Every three months, the Secretary of the Treasury must use all the money in the fund to buy back government bonds and other debt that the public owns. Every three months, the Secretary must send a report to Congress showing how much money was put into the fund and used to pay down debt. The report must list which government bonds or other debts were paid off and how much the total federal debt was reduced.
